sourceMovie.title: Now You See Me
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Now You See Me (2013) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Louis Leterrier's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ocean's Eleven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Now You See Me clicked for you, Ocean's Eleven (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Now You See Me?
faq.answer: Try Ocean's Eleven, Now You See Me 2, The Usual Suspects—each shares DNA with Now You See Me while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Now You See Me?
faq.answer: Now You See Me (2013) is best described as Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Now You See Me part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Now You See Me Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
